summary: Iteration-19 sparkling wine capability verification: end-to-end PASS across engine, content, journal, sitemap, and Cellar Brief UI surfaces. Backend pytest 32/32 (11 new iter-19 sparkling tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iter19_sparkling.py + 21 iter-18/17/16 regression tests). Direct DB proofs: (1) ghost_questions table has 54 sparkling rows with active=1, evenly distributed 6-per-WBS across 9.1–9.9, all category='sparkling', all answers ≥200 chars, all 54 answers mention an Australian sparkling region (Tasmania / Yarra Valley / King Valley / Adelaide Hills / Great Southern / Macedon). (2) cellar_journal.wine_type ENUM now includes 'sparkling' (SHOW COLUMNS confirmed enum('red','white','both','unknown','sparkling')). (3) 54 cellar_journal rows with topic_tag='Sparkling & Bubbles' AND wine_type='sparkling' AND source='ghost.seed'; 41 of the 54 rows mention Tasmania or a Tasmania producer (House of Arras / Jansz / Clover Hill / Pipers Brook) in question or full_answer — well above the ≥10 threshold. Backend API proofs: (a) /api/trpc/cellarBrief.latest — Tank 20 (variety 'Sparkling Chardonnay Cuvée', primary_slowing day 8) is routed to sparkling: ghostQuestion.category='sparkling', journalSlug='sparkling-bubbles-when-should-i-press-sparkling-base-fruit-if-i-want-the-brightest-cleanest-wine', grounding chips are 100% white-flow (SOP 13 White Wine Fermentation Temperature Control, SOP 39 Stuck Fermentation, White Wine Bible Ch.3, White Wine Outline C) with ZERO red-flow leakage (no 'Red Wine Fermentation', no 'Pump-Over Protocol', no 'Cap Management', no 'Red Wine Bible' chip). (b) No cross-contamination: Tank 2 Chardonnay primary_active still gets white grounding (SOP 13, White Wine Bible Ch.3) with ghostQuestion.category='nutrition' (not sparkling) and slug='yeast-nutrients-what-s-the-right-dose-of-dap-for-a-healthy-white-juice-ferment'; Tank 3 Shiraz still gets red-flow grounding (SOP 11/12/14 + Red Wine Bible). (c) /api/trpc/cellarJournal.list({topic:'Sparkling & Bubbles', limit:60}) → total=54, every row starts with slug 'sparkling-bubbles-' and has topicTag/wineType correct. (d) /api/trpc/cellarJournal.getBySlug for a sparkling entry returns source='ghost.seed', wineType='sparkling', fullAnswer ≥200 chars mentioning Tasmania+Jansz+Clover Hill+House of Arras+Yarra Valley+Great Southern WA. (e) /api/sitemap.xml → HTTP 200, content-type application/xml, 308 <loc> tags (>=305 required, up from 254 pre-sparkling). Frontend Playwright PASS on /cellar-brief mobile 430×1400: Tank 20 card renders with TRACKING green pill, 'Sparkling Chardonnay Cuvée' variety, 'Primary slowing · day 8' stage chip, 'Brix 2.5 · ferment finishing (2.8°Bx/day)' trajectory, DECISION DUE banner 'Press window open — confirm dryness + bench taste before pressing', Today's Work checklist ('Daily Brix to confirm <1°Bx before pressing/racking', 'Taste sample — confirm sugars consumed'), GROUNDED IN chips 100% white-flow (SOP 13, SOP 39, White Wine Bible Ch.3, White Wine Outline C), expanded 'Worth Knowing' block with sparkling question 'When should I press sparkling base fruit...?' + full answer visibly mentioning Tasmania / Jansz / Clover Hill / House of Arras / Yarra Valley / Great Southern WA, and 'Read full answer + citations →' link data-testid='brief-ghost-readmore-tank-20-sparkling-chardonnay-cuv-e' with href='/cellar-journal/sparkling-bubbles-when-should-i-press-sparkling-base-fruit-if-i-want-the-brightest-cleanest-wine?from=cellar-brief'.
action_items: (Carry-over from iter_18) cellarJournal.list rows still omit `source` and `teaserAnswer` from the SELECT — detail-only fields via getBySlug. Not blocking; only spec-vs-impl drift.

(Carry-over from iter_16) cellarBrief.history pagination via beforeGeneratedAt cursor still has the duplicate wineryId AND clause instead of lt(generatedAt, cursor). Not exercised in iter-19 but noted.
critical_code_review_comments: server/cellarBriefEngine.ts sparkling branch cleanly filters red-only SOPs and prefers white-flow bibles — Tank 20 grounding chips are 100% white with zero red-flow leakage. Verify in code review that SPARKLING_HINTS/SPARKLING_VARIETIES set covers all industry-standard synonyms (looks good: 'sparkling', 'cuvée', 'blanc de blancs', 'pét-nat', 'prosecco', 'meunier', 'pinot meunier', 'glera' all inferred correctly).

server/cellarBriefEngine.ts inferWineColor correctly probes sparkling FIRST — 'Sparkling Chardonnay Cuvée' → sparkling, 'Chardonnay' alone → white, 'Pinot Noir' alone → red, no regression on still-wine cards.

scripts/seed-sparkling-ghost-questions.mjs produced strong LLM output: all 54 rows have ≥200 char answers, evenly balanced 6-per-WBS across 9.1–9.9, and every single answer contains an Australian region — no low-quality dropouts.

scripts/migrate-ghost-questions-to-journal.mjs correctly mapped sparkling GQ rows to cellar_journal with topic_tag='Sparkling & Bubbles' AND wine_type='sparkling' AND source='ghost.seed'. Sitemap and RSS surfaces pick them up automatically (+54 → 308 <loc> tags).
drizzle/schema.ts cellar_journal.wineType enum update to include 'sparkling' matches live DB SHOW COLUMNS output. Idempotent alter script worked as designed.

success_rate.backend: 100% (32/32 pytest: 11 new iter-19 sparkling + 8 iter-18 coherence + 5 iter-17 ghost-question + 8 iter-16 cellar-brief regression)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (Tank 20 sparkling card renders end-to-end: TRACKING pill, variety label, stage chip, decision banner, today's work checklist, all white-flow grounding chips, expanded Worth Knowing block with Tasmania-producer answer, Read-full-answer link with correct sparkling-bubbles-* href)

test_credentials: Auth bypass ON (ENABLE_DEV_BYPASS, seed-owner-001 = winery_id=1). No login required.
seed_data_creation: None. Consumed existing seed: 54 sparkling ghost_questions (9.1–9.9 × 6 each), 54 published cellar_journal rows tagged 'Sparkling & Bubbles', Tank 20 vessel already seeded with Sparkling Chardonnay Cuvée + 12-day Brix ferment history. Total journal corpus now 307 entries; sitemap serves 308 <loc>.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-19 introduced first-class sparkling routing. Engine: cellarBriefEngine.ts WineColor now includes 'sparkling'; inferWineColor probes SPARKLING_HINTS/SPARKLING_VARIETIES FIRST; resolveGrounding filters red-only SOPs when color is white or sparkling and prefers the White Wine Bible; pickGhostQuestion routes to a dedicated 9.x branch. Content: 54 ghost_questions (WBS 9.1–9.9, 6 each) + 54 mirrored cellar_journal entries under topic_tag='Sparkling & Bubbles'. Journal ENUM extended (enum('red','white','both','unknown','sparkling')). Sitemap grew from 254 → 308 <loc>. Tank 20 is the sparkling regression vessel — variety 'Sparkling Chardonnay Cuvée', stage primary_slowing day 8, ghostQuestion.journalSlug starts 'sparkling-bubbles-'. When adding new still-wine features, use the iter-19 pattern: assert grounding chips do NOT contain 'sparkling' or 'blanc de blancs' or 'tirage' vocabulary on still cards, and vice versa on sparkling cards, to catch any cross-contamination early. Journal list is still server-capped at limit=60. `source` and `teaserAnswer` are still detail-only (getBySlug) fields — not in the list row schema.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All 9 verification points from the review request pass: (1) Tank 20 grounding is white-flow with zero red-flow leakage; (2) Tank 20 ghostQuestion.category='sparkling' with sparkling-bubbles-* slug and 'press sparkling base fruit' question mentioning méthode-traditionnelle context; (3) inferWineColor treats sparkling variety strings correctly and does not regress plain 'Chardonnay'/'Pinot Noir'; (4) 54 sparkling ghost_questions, 6-per-WBS across 9.1–9.9, all AU-region grounded; (5) journal list returns 54 sparkling rows all ghost.seed with 41 Tasmania-producer mentions; (6) ENUM includes 'sparkling'; (7) sitemap = 308 <loc>, application/xml, deep-links resolve; (8) Tank 20 UI renders correctly with all expected sub-elements; (9) all iter-16/17/18 regression tests still green.
